name: Twitter/X API v2
what_it_does: Twitter/X API v2 for reading and posting tweets, searching public content, accessing user data, and streaming real-time tweet events.
use_cases: Monitoring brand mentions or keyword trends in real time
Building social media management agents that post and schedule tweets
Sentiment analysis on public conversations about topics
Research and academic data collection on public discourse
Customer support agents that monitor and respond to mentions
not_for: High-volume data collection without the Enterprise tier (free/basic tiers severely limited)
Commercial data resale or use in competing products
best_when: You need to interact with Twitter/X's real-time conversation data for social monitoring, posting, or analysis.
avoid_when: You need large-scale historical data access, or you're on a budget (pricing is steep since 2023 changes).

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 (Bearer token for read-only app auth, user-context for posting). OAuth 1.0a still supported for some endpoints. App-only auth for search and public data. User auth required for posting and DMs.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 1,500 tweets/month write only, very limited read access
pricing.notes: Pricing dramatically increased post-Elon Musk acquisition. Pro ($5K/month) required for meaningful data access. Academic research track discontinued. Free tier barely useful.

performance.rate_limits: Varies dramatically by tier; Basic: 15 reads/15min for search; Pro: 300/15min

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include title, type, and detail. Rate limit errors include x-rate-limit-reset header. Some v2 errors lack detail compared to v1.1.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Tweet creation is not idempotent. Duplicate tweets are rejected with a specific error but create/like/follow are not safe to retry blindly.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are per-15-minute window - agents must track window reset time from response headers
Free tier write-only restriction makes it nearly useless for agents that need to read data
OAuth 2.0 PKCE flow required for user-context posting - Bearer token alone cannot post tweets
Tweet deduplication rejects exact-same-text tweets within a short window - agents retry loops may get stuck
API v1.1 still exists but is being deprecated - avoid building on v1.1 endpoints
